items.title: The latest AI news we announced in May 2026
items.summary: Here are Google’s latest AI updates from May 2026
items.contentExcerpt: Here’s a recap of our biggest AI updates from May, including announcements from Google I/O 2026, the Android Show and Google Health. Google’s May 2026 updates center on the new "agentic" era, featuring the Gemini 3.5 model and Gemini Omni for advanced reasoning and creation. You can now use proactive tools like the updated Gemini app, Universal Cart for shopping, and the new Google Health app to manage your daily tasks more efficiently. Explore these features across new hardware, including th...

items.title: 财政金融“组合拳” 更大力度支持扩内需
items.summary: 中国政府网要闻列表，覆盖国务院、部门政策和公共事务即时发布
items.contentExcerpt: 今年，我国设立1000亿元财政金融协同促内需专项资金，财政金融部门组合运用贷款贴息、融资担保、风险补偿等方式，引导更多资金流向投资和消费重点领域，激发市场活力。
keywords: 部门政策和公共事务即时发

international.label: 国际
international.description: 国际关系、地缘政治、全球监管和跨境市场事件。

items.title: World News in Brief: Millions displaced in South Sudan, global meat supply quadruples, Middle East crisis deepens global hunger
items.summary: Months of fighting and insecurity have forced hundreds of thousands of people to flee their homes in South Sudan’s eastern Jonglei State, triggering “one of the most severe conflict-related displacement emergencies in recent years”, the UN refugee agency (UNHCR) said on Friday.
items.contentExcerpt: Months of fighting and insecurity have forced hundreds of thousands of people to flee their homes in South Sudan’s eastern Jonglei State, triggering “one of the most severe conflict-related displacement emergencies in recent years”, the UN refugee agency (UNHCR) said on Friday.

items.title: ‘The ocean has no boundaries’: Beauty and life in a war zone
items.summary: When US and Israeli forces launched strikes on Iran on 28 February, triggering one of the most serious geopolitical crises in years, the Strait of Hormuz – a narrow channel just 34 kilometres wide at its narrowest point – became a global flashpoint overnight.
items.contentExcerpt: When US and Israeli forces launched strikes on Iran on 28 February, triggering one of the most serious geopolitical crises in years, the Strait of Hormuz – a narrow channel just 34 kilometres wide at its narrowest point – became a global flashpoint overnight.

items.title: Fighting Fire With Fire
items.summary: In fire-prone ecosystems in Australia’s Northern Territory, prescribed burns are lit to minimize the severity of fires later in the season.
items.contentExcerpt: In fire-prone ecosystems in Australia's Northern Territory, prescribed burns are lit to minimize the severity of fires later in the season. In May and June of most years, NASA satellites typically begin to detect large numbers of wildland fires throughout the Top End and Arnhem Land regions of Australia’s Northern Territory. On some days, especially in the afternoon, the blazes can resemble sizable wildfires in satellite imagery, spreading widely and producing expansive smoke plumes. That was...

items.title: 我国服务贸易持续扩能提质
items.summary: 中国政府网要闻列表，覆盖国务院、部门政策和公共事务即时发布
items.contentExcerpt: 今年以来，我国服务贸易规模稳步增长，结构持续向优，服务贸易对外贸发展的支撑作用正在进一步增强。

items.title: 全国夏粮小麦收获进度过半 已收1.84亿亩
items.summary: 中国政府网要闻列表，覆盖国务院、部门政策和公共事务即时发布
items.contentExcerpt: 截至6月5日17时，全国已收获夏粮小麦1.84亿亩，收获进度54.29%。当日收获1803万亩、投入联合收割机23.68万台（去年当日收获1506万亩、投入联合收割机21.07万台）。
impactAreas: 全国夏粮小麦收获进度过半
keywords: 全国夏粮小麦收获进度过半
部门政策和公共事务即时发

items.title: 共同谱写中朝友好崭新篇章——写在习近平总书记对朝鲜进行国事访问之际
items.summary: 中国政府网要闻列表，覆盖国务院、部门政策和公共事务即时发布
items.contentExcerpt: 应朝鲜劳动党总书记、朝鲜民主主义人民共和国国务委员长金正恩邀请，中共中央总书记、中华人民共和国主席习近平将于6月8日至9日对朝鲜民主主义人民共和国进行国事访问。

写在习近平总书记对朝鲜进

items.title: 中华人民共和国和老挝人民民主共和国关于构建新时代全天候中老命运共同体的联合声明
items.summary: 中国政府网要闻列表，覆盖国务院、部门政策和公共事务即时发布
items.contentExcerpt: 双方认为，中国共产党和老挝人革党肩负为人民谋幸福、为国家谋发展、为人类谋进步的历史使命。坚持党的领导和社会主义道路是中老关系的最鲜明本质特征，也是中老关系发展的最坚实政治基础。

items.title: NASA’s X-59 Aircraft Flies Supersonic for First Time
items.summary: NASA’s experimental X-59 aircraft marked a major milestone Friday, June 5, when it flew faster than the speed of sound for the first time, setting the stage for demonstrating its quiet supersonic capabilities later this year.  NASA test pilot Jim “Clue” Less took off and landed at Edwards Air Force Base in California, reaching a top speed of approximately Mach 1.1 (713 mph)&...
items.contentExcerpt: NASA’s experimental X-59 aircraft marked a major milestone Friday, June 5, when it flew faster than the speed of sound for the first time, setting the stage for demonstrating its quiet supersonic capabilities later this year. NASA test pilot Jim “Clue” Less took off and landed at Edwards Air Force Base in California, reaching a top speed of approximately Mach 1.1 (713 mph) and altitude of 43,400 feet. The X-59’s flight began at 11:08 a.m. PDT and lasted 81 minutes, with the team focusing on f...

items.title: NASA Concludes Antenna Mishap Investigation, Releases Report
items.summary: NASA has completed the investigation into the damage sustained last year at its 70-meter radio-frequency antenna, known as the Deep Space Station 14 (DSS-14), at the Goldstone Deep Space Communications Complex near Barstow, California. The agency has classified the event as a Type A mishap based on the total cost of damages. The antenna will […]
items.contentExcerpt: NASA has completed the investigation into the damage sustained last year at its 70-meter radio-frequency antenna, known as the Deep Space Station 14 (DSS-14), at the Goldstone Deep Space Communications Complex near Barstow, California. The agency has classified the event as a Type A mishap based on the total cost of damages. The antenna will remain offline to complete repairs and previously scheduled upgrades. “NASA takes safety and any departure from established procedures seriously, and the...

items.title: First Steps: America’s Grueling Second Spacewalk
items.summary: One year after Gemini IV astronaut Edward H. White completed NASA’s first spacewalk the agency prepared for a demanding second excursion. Originally scheduled for Gemini VIII, the extravehicular activity (EVA) was reassigned to Gemini IX-A after that mission ended early, with Gene Cernan taking on the task. On June 5, 1966—the mission’s third day—Cernan exited […]
items.contentExcerpt: One year after Gemini IV astronaut Edward H. White completed NASA’s first spacewalk the agency prepared for a demanding second excursion. Originally scheduled for Gemini VIII, the extravehicular activity (EVA) was reassigned to Gemini IX-A after that mission ended early, with Gene Cernan taking on the task. On June 5, 1966—the mission’s third day—Cernan exited the spacecraft and quickly found himself fighting his own equipment. His spacesuit was so rigid that even simple movements required in...

items.title: Faith, fear and trust: Inside DR Congo’s fight against Ebola
items.summary: In a village in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), health workers arrived a few days ago to help bury a person who had died from Ebola. Instead, they were threatened, told armed rebels would be called if they stayed, and forced to leave.
items.contentExcerpt: In a village in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), health workers arrived a few days ago to help bury a person who had died from Ebola. Instead, they were threatened, told armed rebels would be called if they stayed, and forced to leave.

items.title: Haiti: Harrowing needs must be met with long-term engagement
items.summary: Escalating gang violence across Haiti has pushed displacement to record levels, deepening an already severe humanitarian crisis and leaving nearly 1.5 million people without a stable place to live, according to new figures released Friday by the UN migration agency, IOM.
items.contentExcerpt: Escalating gang violence across Haiti has pushed displacement to record levels, deepening an already severe humanitarian crisis and leaving nearly 1.5 million people without a stable place to live, according to new figures released Friday by the UN migration agency, IOM.

items.title: NASA Announces Winners of 2026 University Innovation Competition
items.summary: NASA announced the Massachusetts Institute of Technology project, Exploration-Class Lunar Integrated Power SystEm, as the first place winner for the 2026 Revolutionary Aerospace Systems Concepts – Academic Linkage (RASC-AL) competition, which challenges students to bridge gaps in aerospace technology by innovating new system concepts and prototypes. Another team from the same university won second place overall for their project, Mars Exploration Layered Infrastructur...
items.contentExcerpt: NASA announced the Massachusetts Institute of Technology project, Exploration-Class Lunar Integrated Power SystEm, as the first place winner for the 2026 Revolutionary Aerospace Systems Concepts – Academic Linkage (RASC-AL) competition, which challenges students to bridge gaps in aerospace technology by innovating new system concepts and prototypes. Another team from the same university won second place overall for their project, Mars Exploration Layered Infrastructure for Operations, Researc...
tags: Center of Excellence for Collaborative Innovation (CoECI)

items.title: NASA’s Artemis II Moon Mission Research Continues on Earth
items.summary: Since NASA’s Artemis II crew members safely splashed down in the Pacific Ocean on April 10 after their record-setting mission around the Moon, science teams have been busy collecting more data and combing through observations collected on the test flight. Results from these science investigations will help support safe human exploration of deep space and […]
items.contentExcerpt: Since NASA’s Artemis II crew members safely splashed down in the Pacific Ocean on April 10 after their record-setting mission around the Moon, science teams have been busy collecting more data and combing through observations collected on the test flight. Results from these science investigations will help support safe human exploration of deep space and provide a blueprint for how future missions will conduct science on the lunar surface as NASA builds a Moon Base and develops an enduring hu...

items.title: ‘The true cost of peace’: UN honours fallen peacekeepers as dangers mount
items.summary: The United Nations paused on Friday to pay tribute to the more than 4,500 peacekeepers who lost their lives in the line of duty over the past 78 years.
items.contentExcerpt: The United Nations paused on Friday to pay tribute to the more than 4,500 peacekeepers who lost their lives in the line of duty over the past 78 years.

items.title: Lebanon crisis: Needs soar as UN launches new funding appeal
items.summary: The UN in Lebanon appealed for an additional $331.5 million on Friday to help 1.4 million people in crisis as already massive needs continue to grow, three months since deadly violence erupted between Hezbollah fighters and Israeli forces.
items.contentExcerpt: The UN in Lebanon appealed for an additional $331.5 million on Friday to help 1.4 million people in crisis as already massive needs continue to grow, three months since deadly violence erupted between Hezbollah fighters and Israeli forces.
